Home/Atlas/Insight Blocks Test

Insight Blocks Showcase

This page demonstrates all four insight block types with consistent iconography and styling.

Tip Block

Pro Tip: Use the Search Feature

Press / or Ctrl+K to instantly search through all pages in the Atlas. The search ranks results by title relevance, making it easy to find exactly what you need.

Tips are highlighted in yellow and use a lightbulb icon to indicate helpful advice and best practices.

Warning Block

Common Pitfall: Breaking Changes

When upgrading dependencies, always check the changelog for breaking changes. Test your code thoroughly before deploying to production.

Warnings are highlighted in orange and use an alert triangle icon to catch user attention.

Important Block

Critical: Security Configuration

Never commit secrets or API keys to version control. Always use environment variables or secure secret management tools. This is a critical security requirement.

Important blocks are highlighted in red and use a lightning bolt icon for critical information.

Takeaway Block

Key Takeaway: Consistency is Key

The most important aspect of code design is consistency. Whether you choose tabs or spaces, camelCase or snake_case, make sure the choice is consistent throughout your project.

Takeaways are highlighted in purple and use a star icon to emphasize key points and main concepts.

Mixed Content with Insights

Insight blocks work well alongside other content types:

Documentation Best Practice

Always include examples in your documentation. Code snippets are more valuable than lengthy explanations.
  • Use clear, descriptive variable names
  • Add comments for complex logic
  • Keep functions small and focused
  • Write unit tests for critical paths

Testing Requirements

All new features must have corresponding unit tests. Aim for at least 80% code coverage on critical paths.
